key_insights.insight: Narcissistic relationships can be as damaging as relationships with addicts, but are often harder to recognize and validate.
key_insights.quote: The experience people have in narcissistic relationships in a way is no different. With the added bit though, at least with addiction, people can say, I see what the behavior is. I see what the issue is.

key_insights.insight: Narcissists often display different behaviors in public versus private, making it difficult for outsiders to recognize the abuse.
key_insights.quote: They can go out in the world and they're able to be charming and charismatic and confident and smart and the center of attention and running companies. And behind closed doors, they psychologically eviscerate the people they're with.

key_insights.insight: A key difference between someone having a bad day and a narcissist is the ability to take accountability and make amends.
key_insights.quote: When people are not narcissistic and they have bad days, they will take accountability, they will make amends and they will change their behavior and say, I'm not doing this again.

key_insights.insight: Narcissists possess a wider behavioral repertoire, able to be extremely charming in certain situations while being abusive in others, unlike someone who is consistently an 'asshole'.
key_insights.quote: This is a person who can be absolutely charming on the golf course with the CEO of their company...and get home, forget it was his anniversary, scream at their partner.

key_insights.insight: While narcissists might make small behavioral changes in therapy, it often isn't enough to repair the damage caused to their relationships.
key_insights.quote: These micro changes, and they are micro changes, but they are changes and they're in the right direction. That much water under the bridge for the family members and partners and other people that have been harmed.

key_insights.insight: Gaslighting is a tactic that is predicated on a relationship characterized by trust.
key_insights.quote: Gaslighting is predicated on a relationship that's ostensibly characterized by trust. So that's why strangers can't gaslight you in the same way as an intimate partner, a trusted colleague, a family member.

key_insights.insight: Gaslighting is not about evidence or truth; it is about overpowering the other person and making them doubt their own reality.
key_insights.quote: This isn't about evidence. This is about them overpowering you.

key_insights.insight: DARVO (Deny, Attack, Reverse Victim and Offender) is a tactic used by narcissists to shut down conversations and reverse blame.
key_insights.quote: What the narcissistic person will in a very skilled, I mean, in a cruelly skillful way do is if the person, the gaslighted person ever attempts to push back on something that the narcissist has done...Reverse victim and offender.

actionable_advice.advice: When being gaslighted, disengage from the conversation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Recognize the denial of your reality, state 'We're having a different experience then,' and avoid further engagement. Do not try to prove them wrong with evidence.

memorable_stories: The guest recounts being gaslighted in a professional corporate setting, highlighting how even well-intentioned people can perpetuate gaslighting to protect a larger organizational structure.
contrarian_claims: Assholery and Narcissism, while often used interchangeably, are different; Narcissists can be charming where an asshole is consistently an asshole.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Most Replayed Moment: How to Know If You're Being Gaslit by a Narcissist And What to Do About It: Dr Ramani Durvasula
